criteria.criterion: Criterion A (Hypomanic Episode): A distinct period of abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood AND abnormally and persistently increased activity or energy, lasting at least 4 consecutive days, present most of the day, nearly every day.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion B (Hypomanic Episode): ≥3 symptoms (≥4 if mood only irritable) from the 7-symptom list, persisting, representing a noticeable change from usual behavior, and present to a significant degree.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion C (Hypomanic Episode): The episode is associated with an unequivocal change in functioning that is uncharacteristic of the individual when not symptomatic.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion D (Hypomanic Episode): The disturbance in mood and the change in functioning are observable by others.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion E (Hypomanic Episode): The episode is NOT severe enough to cause marked impairment in social or occupational functioning or to necessitate hospitalization.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion A (Disorder): Criteria have been met for at least one hypomanic episode and at least one major depressive episode.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion B (Disorder): There has never been a manic episode.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion D (Disorder): The symptoms of depression OR the unpredictability caused by frequent alternation between periods of depression and hypomania cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion A (Major Depressive Episode): ≥5 of 9 symptoms during the same 2-week period, representing a change from prior functioning. At least one symptom must be (1) depressed mood or (2) loss of interest or pleasure (anhedonia). Symptoms: 1. Depressed mood most of the day... 6. Fatigue or loss of energy...
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day.

criteria.criterion: Criterion B (Major Depressive Episode): The symptoms cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day.

criteria.criterion: Criterion C (Major Depressive Episode): The episode is not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day.

criteria.criterion: Criterion E (Disorder): There has never been a manic or hypomanic episode (unless these were substance-induced or attributable to a medical condition).
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion A: Excessive anxiety and worry (apprehensive expectation), occurring more days than not for at least 6 months, about a number of events or activities (e.g., work or school performance).
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion B: The individual finds it difficult to control the worry.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion C: The anxiety and worry are associated with ≥3 of the following 6 symptoms (with at least some symptoms present more days than not for the past 6 months): 1. Restlessness or feeling keyed up or on edge. 2. Being easily fatigued. 3. Difficulty concentrating or mind going blank. 4. Irritability. 5. Muscle tension. 6. Sleep disturbance...
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion D: The anxiety, worry, or physical symptoms cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion E: Not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance (drug of abuse, medication) or another medical condition (e.g., hyperthyroidism).
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.

criteria.criterion: Criterion F: Not better explained by another mental disorder.
criteria.evidence: Patient has been feeling pretty lethargic and angry for the vast majority of the day. Patient has noticed that they've been talking faster than they typically do.
